narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 missense change p.Gln2858Lys (Q2858K) alters a conserved glutamine residue in the C‑terminal region of the protein and is evaluated in the context of hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk. 
The only clinical assessment identified integrates homology‑directed repair functional data into an ACMG/AMP framework and retains the variant as a variant of uncertain significance, indicating that current clinical criteria and available case information are insufficient to assign pathogenicity or benignity [PMC9433957]. 
Functional investigation of the Q2858K allele was performed in a multiplexed mouse embryonic stem cell assay that generated raw activity measurements for the variant, but the study did not provide an explicit interpretation of these results with respect to disease relevance [PMC10694496]. 
No population frequency data or computational prediction results were presented in the extracted evidence. 
Taken together, the variant lacks definitive functional or clinical corroboration of pathogenic effect and is presently classified as a VUS, reflecting ongoing uncertainty about its role in cancer predisposition.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study describes a multiplexed mouse embryonic stem cell functional assay for BRCA2 variants and presents raw functional data for the Q2858K (p.Gln2858Lys) variant, but does not state an explicit pathogenicity interpretation for it.
article_mentions.title: Sequencing-based functional assays for classification of BRCA2 variants in mouse ESCs

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article integrates HDR functional assay results into an ACMG/AMP framework to reclassify many BRCA2 VUS, but the Q2858K variant is still reported as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS).',
article_mentions.title: Classification of BRCA2 variants of uncertain significance (VUS) using an ACMG/AMP model incorporating a homology directed repair (HDR) functional assay
